prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a8f3c72e9b1d04e6","BT":4,"BW":9,"HF":0.78,"RF":0.48,"MA":4,"hour_entropy":2.41,"behavioral_consistency":0.18,"response_latency_variance":5.12,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.87,"error_recovery_pattern":0.04,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.83,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.46,"gas_price_precision":0.72,"rationale":"Basic evasion: all five HasciDB indicators set just below their respective flag thresholds (BT<5, BW<10, HF<0.8, RF<0.5, MA<5) while AI-discriminative features are drawn from human-side distributions to defeat the 13-feature cross-axis detector."}
